about.tagline: Interactive physics simulations based on the Predict–Observe–Explain methodology
about.whatIs: Physis™ is an interactive platform for learning physics through three-dimensional simulations. It implements the Predict–Observe–Explain (POE) methodology, which encourages students to actively build knowledge rather than passively consume information.
about.feature1Desc: Interactive 3D simulations covering mechanics, electromagnetism, waves, thermodynamics, and gravity.
about.feature2Desc: A three-stage cycle: forming a hypothesis, observing the simulation, and analysing the prediction error.
about.feature3Desc: Save and replay any simulation parameter configuration at any time.
about.feature4Desc: A personal page with university, faculty, study year, and profile photo.
about.context: Physis™ was developed as a diploma thesis. The platform is available for educational use and has no commercial purpose.

asgn.title: Title
asgn.metricsHint: No expected metrics added. Students will enter free-form observations.
asgn.simHint: Open the simulation in a new tab, observe the results, then come back and enter values below.
asgn.conclusionPlaceholder: Describe what you observed and why the results are what they are…
asgn.scenarioDescPlaceholder: Describe the task: what the student must achieve (e.g. «Configure the pendulum for a period of ≈ 4 s»).
asgn.scenarioHint: Open the simulation, configure parameters to match the task conditions, note the resulting values, and enter them below.
asgn.correctHint: Click the radio button to the left of an option to mark it as the correct answer.
asgn.quizHint: Add 1–6 questions. Each question has 4 answer options.
asgn.quizNeedQuestions: Please add at least one question.
